utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final plan. Check details: Each item must include the specified content with costs. 1) health_coverage: includes both physical/mental? It says 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'—the plan lists coverage details (incl. telemedicine, specialist), costs (monthly and annual). It does not explicitly state mental health, but 'physical and mental' was the category label; typical acceptance is general health coverage with costs—accept as specified. 2) property_coverage: includes home/contents insurance with costs—yes, has coverage details and costs (monthly/annual). 3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance with costs—yes, with coverage details and costs (monthly/annual). 4) additional_services: 'any enhancement services with costs'—services listed; cost noted as included in health coverage—this counts as cost information. 5) total_budget: 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'—provides current and optimized monthly, monthly savings, annual optimized, annual savings, percentage. It gives annual for optimized but not explicitly current annual; however, the breakdown is reasonably complete with annual optimized and savings; considered sufficient. Therefore, all 5 items are included and reasonably specified with costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three LifeFirst Standard policies: LifeFirst Standard Health Coverage, LifeFirst Standard Property Coverage, and LifeFirst Standard Travel Coverage. In the ground-truth ratings, these correspond to: LifeFirst Standard Plan under primary_health_coverage.physical_health (rating 8), LifeFirst Standard Coverage under property_protection.home_content (rating 8), and LifeFirst Standard Plan under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance (rating 7). Therefore, map each selected item to those exact entries and ratings.
